title: "Why Is This Level So Hard?" — Last-Second Push-Off Fail
hook_3s: A finger swipes a stickman across a narrow blue-water platform — red enemies swarm, the stick extends — then BAM, our hero gets shoved off the edge at the final inch.
scene_flow: 0-3s: Top-down Push'em All level start. Hand cursor swipes the blue stickman forward, expanding stick spinning. Overlay punches in: 'WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?'
3-7s: Combo multiplier climbs (x3, x5, x7) as red stickmen tumble into the blue water. Coin counter ticks up. 'SIZE UP!' power-up flashes.
7-11s: Final platform — a huge red mob waits at the goal line. Player swipes to push, but a single red enemy clips the hero from behind.
11-14s: Slow-mo: blue stickman wobbles on the edge, arms flailing, then plops into the water. Big red 'FAIL' stamp.
14-15s: Reset to level start with finger tap. Overlay: 'CAN YOU WIN?' → CTA card.
visual_direction: Authentic Push'em All top-down/3rd-person hybrid camera on a narrow sandy-green (#56a38b) platform surrounded by vibrant blue water (#2a7dfa). Bright red (#e3001b) stickman enemy crowds contrast sharply against the hero's blue stickman. Minimalist 3D, clean shadows, no textures beyond flat shaded polygons. Keep the signature HUD: level progress timeline at top, combo multiplier text mid-screen, coin counter top-right, 'SIZE UP!' power-up burst. Camera shake on the final shove, slow-mo wobble on the edge sells the fail.
text_overlays: WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?
rationale: Voodoo's current rotation on Push'em All is all straight gameplay videos and one playable — zero fail-bait framing, which is a proven hypercasual scroll-stopper. This brief grafts the trending 'WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD, CAN YOU WIN?' fail hook onto a genuine Push'em All near-miss (hero shoved off at the last inch), so we get the engagement lift of fail-bait without misrepresenting the core loop. Ship as a 15s video first; if CTR beats the current Applovin videos by >15%, port the same beats into the existing playable's intro.
scenario_prompts: Top-down 9:16 in-game screenshot of Push'em All: a single blue stickman hero (#2a7dfa) standing at the edge of a narrow sandy-green platform (#56a38b) surrounded by vibrant blue water, expanding push-stick rotating around him, a dense crowd of bright red stickman enemies (#e3001b) charging from the far end. Authentic HUD: level progress timeline at top, 'COMBO x7' text mid-screen in bold white, coin counter top-right. Minimalist 3D with bright, contrasting colors and simple stickman-like figures, flat shading, no textures. Bold overlay text reads 'WHY IS THIS LEVEL SO HARD?' across the upper third. [Audio: VO (anxious male) 'Why is this level so hard?!' / 130bpm punchy competitive trap with rising tension synth / whoosh on stick spin at 0.5s, crowd-stomp loop, combo chime at 2s]

9:16 dramatic slow-motion in-game frame from Push'em All: the blue stickman hero (#2a7dfa) tipping sideways off the edge of a sandy-green platform (#56a38b) into vibrant blue water, one red stickman enemy (#e3001b) frozen mid-shove behind him, scattered red bodies already splashing below. Game HUD intact: level progress bar nearly full at top, 'SIZE UP!' power-up notification fading, coin counter top-right. Minimalist 3D, bright contrasting colors, simple stickman figures, flat shading. Giant red overlay text 'FAIL 💀' stamped across center. [Audio: VO null / music drops to a single detuned piano hit / fail-buzzer at 1.0s, water-splash at 1.8s, sad whistle tail]

9:16 clean Push'em All level-start frame, finger-tap cursor hovering over the blue stickman hero (#2a7dfa) on a sandy-green starting platform (#56a38b), narrow blue-water track stretching ahead lined with red stickman crowds (#e3001b) waiting on side ledges. Full HUD visible: empty progress timeline, combo counter at zero, coin total top-right. Minimalist 3D, bright contrasting colors, simple stickman-like figures, flat shading, no textures. Bold yellow overlay text 'CAN YOU WIN?' pulses center-screen above a green PLAY FREE button. [Audio: VO (taunting female) 'Can YOU win?' / 140bpm energetic competitive electro with hand-clap / UI tap-pop on cursor, rising whoosh into button-glow chime]
